_comment: Journal quality tiers + publisher patterns + warning patterns. Used by JOURNAL-QUALITY-FILTER.ps1 (post-results filter). Source data comes from Beall's List archive (https://beallslist.net) and the local predatory_journals.json file.

tier_1_trusted.description: Indexed in Web of Science, Scopus, PubMed/MEDLINE, IEEE Xplore, ACM DL. Keep always.

tier_2_open_access.description: Indexed in DOAJ, PubMed Central, CORE. Keep with badge.

tier_3_unverified.description: Not matched to Tier 1 or 2. Show with warning badge, do not remove.

tier_4_predatory.description: Match against Beall's List archive. Hide by default, show with red badge if toggled.
tier_4_predatory.warning_patterns_note: These are warning flags, not automatic blocks. Assign Tier 3 if no other tier confirmed.

quality_scoring._comment: Applied AFTER tier assignment. Sums to a quality bonus that is added to the relevance score from KEYWORD-FILTER.ps1.

implementation_notes.1: Do not hardcode a full journal whitelist locally. Use the OpenAlex is_in_doaj field as the live DOAJ check. Use publisher name matching for Tier 1 (Elsevier, IEEE etc).
implementation_notes.2: Beall's List cannot be queried via API. Hardcode the most common flagged journal name strings as a local blocklist array. Update manually when needed.
implementation_notes.3: arXiv papers are legitimate but not peer reviewed. Always flag them as preprints. Never assign Tier 1 or 2.
implementation_notes.4: Citation count comes from Semantic Scholar citationCount field. Use it directly in scoring.
implementation_notes.5: Never auto-remove Tier 3 results. Researchers sometimes need grey literature. Always let the user see unverified results with a clear warning.
